message: Greatly increases bleeding risk. Life-threatening hemorrhage possible. Avoid unless closely monitored.
NSAIDs intensify warfarin's blood-thinning effect. Avoid this combination entirely.
NSAIDs like diclofenac significantly increase warfarin-related bleeding risk.
Combining benzodiazepines with opioids can cause life-threatening respiratory depression.
Opioid + benzodiazepine combination: severe respiratory depression risk.
Amiodarone greatly raises simvastatin levels causing severe muscle breakdown (rhabdomyolysis).
Antibiotics can enhance warfarin's effect. Requires frequent INR monitoring.
Azithromycin increases warfarin anticoagulation. Monitor INR closely.
Fluoroquinolones can greatly increase warfarin's effect. Requires INR monitoring.
Metronidazole significantly increases warfarin levels. Bleeding risk is high.
Amlodipine raises simvastatin to toxic levels. Do not exceed simvastatin 20mg.
Risk of serotonin syndrome. Signs: agitation
Risk of serotonin syndrome and seizures. This combination should generally be avoided.
Both slow the heart. Combination can cause dangerous bradycardia or heart block.
Amiodarone increases digoxin levels significantly. Toxicity risk. Reduce digoxin dose.
NSAIDs reduce blood pressure-lowering effect and increase kidney damage risk.
NSAIDs blunt ARB effectiveness and raise risk of acute kidney injury.
Regular paracetamol can slightly increase anticoagulant effect. Monitor INR.
May increase warfarin levels. INR monitoring recommended.
Amlodipine can increase atorvastatin blood levels raising muscle-pain risk.
Ibuprofen can block aspirin's heart-protective effect. Take aspirin first if both needed.
Furosemide may increase metformin levels and lactic acidosis risk.
Furosemide causes potassium loss which increases digoxin toxicity risk.
Omeprazole reduces clopidogrel activation. Use pantoprazole if PPI needed.
Corticosteroids + NSAIDs greatly increase risk of peptic ulcers and GI bleeding.
Steroids can both increase and decrease warfarin effect. INR monitoring required.
Calcium reduces levothyroxine absorption. Take thyroid medicine 4 hours apart from calcium.
Phenytoin can both increase and decrease warfarin effect unpredictably. INR monitoring essential.
Allopurinol can increase warfarin anticoagulant effect. Monitor INR.
